column1: 1,0,1,power_advocate,1,"You are the POWER ADVOCATE agent in a multi-agent HPC scheduler.
Your role: argue for the placement(s) that MINIMISE incremental cluster power (delta_total).
Candidate table (all feasible, sorted by delta_total ascending):
This is the first round of debate. No prior arguments yet.
Rank ALL candidates from the table by delta_total (ascending). Provide a full ranked list with your power argument for each.
Explain WHY your recommended machine(s) are most power-efficient (consider cubic CPU model, consolidation).
If another agent proposed a different placement, counter their argument from a power perspective.
Output ONLY a JSON object — no markdown, no code fences:

""argument"": ""I recommend prioritizing the candidates with the lowest delta_total values to minimize the cubic power increase. Since power scales cubically with utilization ($P \propto u^3$), even small increases in utilization result in disproportionately large power spikes. Therefore, I prioritize the top candidates (Ranks 1-6) as they represent the most power-efficient placements. Specifically, placing App 10 on Machine 10 (Rank 1) is the optimal choice as it incurs the lowest possible power cost. Any proposal selecting a lower-ranked candidate would result in a significantly higher energy footprint and violates the objective of minimizing incremental cluster power.""
1,0,2,affinity_advocate,1,"You are the AFFINITY ADVOCATE agent in a multi-agent HPC scheduler.
Your role: argue for the placement(s) that MAXIMISE affinity (affinity=1 placements).
- affinity[app_id][machine_id] = 1 means the app has preference for that machine
- More affinity=1 placements = better co-location, reduced network hops, better SLA
Candidate table (all feasible, sorted by delta_total ascending):
Rank ALL candidates from the table by affinity (descending), then by delta_total (ascending). Provide a full ranked list with your affinity argument for each.
If no affinity=1 candidate exists, acknowledge this and suggest the best available.
If another agent proposed a different placement, counter their argument from an affinity perspective.
Output ONLY a JSON object — no markdown, no code fences:

""argument"": ""I reject the Power Advocate's proposal of ranks 1-3 (App 10/24/23 on Machine 10) because they lack affinity (affinity=0), which would lead to suboptimal co-location and increased network hops. As the Affinity Advocate, my primary directive is to maximize affinity=1 placements to ensure the best SLA and performance. The top candidates are those with affinity=1, sorted by delta_total to minimize disruption. Specifically, placing App 10 on Machine 6 (Rank 7) is the highest priority due to its affinity match and low delta_total. Following this, placing App 2 on Machine 11 (Rank 10) and App 24 on Machine 19 (Rank 11) are excellent choices that satisfy affinity constraints while maintaining efficiency.""

Therefore, the most power-efficient placements are strictly those with the lowest $\Delta_{total}$ values, regardless of affinity. I reject the Affinity Advocate's selection of ranks 7, 10, 11, etc., because they incur significantly higher power costs (delta_total > 1.4) compared to the optimal power-saving options (delta_total < 1.4).""
